dialog: Eminence...
Janusz, your presence made him so happy.
Subvenite, Sancti Dei...
... occurrite, Angeli Domini, Suscipientes animam eius. Offerentes eam in conspectu Altissimi...
Suscipiat te Christus qui vocavit te et in sinum Abrahae angeli deducant te.
Sicut erat in principio et nunc et semper et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.
He is with God.
Archbishop Wozniak.
Sede vacante. The throne of the Holy See is vacant.
Aldo...
Do you think anyone would m ind if I took this, Thomas? As a keepsake?
Of course. Take it. He would have wanted you to have it.
We used to play quite often at the end of the day. He said it helped him relax.
Who won?
Oh, he did. Always eight moves ahead.
Ah, Aldo, I’m so sorry. What happened, do you know?
They say heart attack. There had been warnings.
I hadn’t heard that.
He didn’t want anyone to know. He thought if word got out they would start spreading rumours he was going to resig n.
The Curia.
We’ll have to be careful what we say to the media about his condition.
Janusz, I know this is hard for you, but we’ll need to prepare a detailed statement. Who discovered the Holy Father’s body?
I did, Your Eminence.
Well, thank God, that’s something. What did you do?
I called the Holy Father’s doctor. He always spent the night in the room next door. But... it was too late.
What time was this?
Around eleven thirty, Eminence. I would have called you sooner, but...
Thomas, I’m so sorry. I know His Holiness had no closer colleagues than you and Aldo... But I asked Janusz to hold off calling you. I... I wanted to ascertain all the facts.
Well, I’m sure you acted for the best.
The way rumours can spread. One and a quarter billion souls watching.
I’ve prepared a time-line of His Holiness’ last day.
The Holy Father’s most recent medical records. He had an angiogram last month.
You can see the evidence of a blockage...just here.
Joe, perhaps...perhaps we could release the data, but not the photograph? It feels too...too....
I know, Thomas, I know. But there will have to be an autopsy.
The time before Vespers? What was he doing then?
Routine meetings as far as I understand it.
Who was the last to have a scheduled meeting with him?
I believe that may have been me. I saw him at four. Is that right Janusz? Was I the last?
You were, Eminence.
Put in all the meetings he had that day. It will show how hard he was working, right up to the end.
That might look as if we were placing a huge burden on a sick man.
The Papacy is a huge burden. Especially for an older man.
Has anyone telephoned Cardinal Tedesco?
As a courtesy.
Courtesy? What has he ever done to deserve courtesy? If anyone can be said to have killed the Holy Father, he did!
I have to ring him, Aldo.
Tedesco.
He already knew.
Goffredo? It’s Lawrence. I’m afraid I have terrible news. The Holy Father has just passed away
Goffredo? Sono Lawrence. Purtroppo devo comunicarvi una terribile notizia. Il Santo Padre è appena deceduto.
Your Eminence?
Eminenza?
Thank you, Lawrence. I shall pray for his soul.
Grazie, Lawrence. Pregheròper la sua anima
He already knew.
Of course he knew. This place is full of his supporters. He probably knew before we did.
It’s trending. The rumours that the Pope is dead are trending. On the internet. We should move quickly or we’ll fall behind the news cycle.
We should wait until daylight. The dignity of the office requires...
For God’s sake, Joshua. When did the Holy Father ever care about the “dignity of the office?” Look at h is rooms.
The body should be embalmed. Remember Pius XII? Went off like a firecracker in his coffin...
Well, Dean. The responsibility for the Conclave falls upon you.
Security say they’d like to test the electronic jammers one last time, your Eminence.
Then they’d better be quick. Is this really necessary, Willi?
Apparently so. Security say.. ...eavesdroppers can use lasers to “read the vibrations on the glass?”
Let’s hope none of our brothers suffer from claustrophobia. Who knows how long we’ll have to be in here.
I assume we are going to finish in time?
They will work through the night if they have to. It will be fine, Eminence. It always is. Italy, you know.
Sorry to fuss.
Well, Eminence, I’d say this is a pretty fair vision of hell.
Don’t be blasphemous, Ray. Hell arrives tomorrow when we bring in the cardinals.
How on earth does one pronounce this? Salso?
Kahl-koh, Eminence. He’s Indian.
Kahl-koh. Thank you Willi.
Archbishop Wozniak has asked if he may have a word, Eminence?
I don’t think that’s possible. The cardinals will begin arriving in an hour. What’s it about?
He didn’t say. . I wouldn’t have mentioned it but he seemed so...upset?
We’re going to be sequestered from six o’clock. He should have come earlier.
Yes, your Eminence. I’ll tell him.
Ray? Tell him I’ll see him after I’ve met the cardinals. The poor fellow will be worrying about his future.
Getting puffed up with my own importance, Willi.
The Conclave will be over in a few days and then no-one will be interested in me.
Shelter from the Storm.
Dear Lord, that’s enormous.
Apparently Pope John the Twenty- Third was too fat to fit into the biggest cassock. They had to split the seam in the back for him to get into it.
You look tired.
All this... it’s a duty I never thought I’d have to perform. I always assumed he would out-live us all.
We... we didn’t part well.
I asked for his permission to retire as Dean. Join an order. I have been having... difficulties.
What did he say?
He refused my resignation. Said that some were chosen to be Shepherds, and some to manage the farm. Apparently, I’m a manager.
Things were a little cold between us when I left. . And that was the last time I saw him.
The Holy Father told me of your... crisis of faith. He said you had difficulties with prayer.
You know that he had doubts himself, by the end?
The Pope had doubts about God?
Never about God. What he had lost faith in was the Church.
Here they come, Your Eminence.
Brothers, welcome.
Are we criminals now? Searched? Luggage opened...
I’m so sorry, Your Eminence, but we are told there is a heightened state of security.
And look at this. Spat upon. The Protestors. We’re all paedophiles now, apparently. Don Tutino?
Will not be attending.
Eminence Khal-Koh, welcome!
Thomas!
Is he smuggling in his private Chef?
How many is that, Willi?
One hundred and three, Eminence.
I wonder where Tedesco has got to.
Perhaps he isn’t coming?
That would be too much to hope for.
We can wait inside if you prefer?
No, let’s get some fresh air while we still can.
Am I the last?
Not quite. How are you?
Oh fairly dreadful. You’ve seen the papers? Apparently it’s already decided it’s to be me.
And I happen to agree with them.
What if I don’t want it? No sane man would want the papacy.
Some of our colleagues seem to.
But what if I know in my heart I’m not worthy?
You’re more worthy than any of us.
I’m not.
Then... t ell your supporters not to vote for you. Pass the chalice...
And let it go to him? How am I to live with myself if I don’t try to stop him?
Apologies, Lawrence. My train was delayed in Venice.
ather Tedesco. We’ve missed you.
No doubt. But don’t worry, my friends have kept me wellinformed. >
Are you well? >
Ah, no-one is ever well at our age. How have you found your new responsibilities? You have everything undercontrol?
I believe so.
Good.  Stop hovering. I carry my own bag.
And let it go to him? How am I to live with myself if I don’t try to stop him?
Heavenly Father, Bless this Conclave, and guide it in Wisdom.
Where is he?
The meeting room, Your Eminence.
Will you take these upstairs for me?
Do you want me to sit in?
No, no, I’ll deal with it.
Janusz?
In the name of the Father, the Son and the Holy Ghost. My last confession was four weeks ago...
Janusz, Janusz, I’m sorry I don’t have time to hear your confession. There is so much still to do.
Have you been drinking?
What’s troubling you? Tell me.
I should have come to you before. But I promised I wouldn’t say anything.
Promised who?
Cardinal Tremblay.
Janusz, the doors close soon and you’ll have to leave. Now if you promised Father Tremblay then perhaps it isn’t right for you to...
The day the Pope died, the last person to have an official appointment with him was Cardinal Tremblay.
Yes, I know. It’s on the official timeline for...
At that meeting, the Holy Father dismissed him from all his offices in the Church.
What?
He sacked him.
Why?
For gross misconduct.
You...you tell me this now? We are about to be sequestered and...
Forgive me! But it wasn’t until the last few days, when I started to hear the rumours...
What rumours?
That he might be elected Pope.
And you see it as your duty to prevent that, do you?
I no l onger know what my duty is.
Were you there at this meeting?
No, Your Eminence. The Holy Father told me about it afterwards, when we had supper.
Did he tell you why he had dismissed Father Tremblay?
No. He said the reasons would become clear soon enough. He was very angry .
Does anyone else know about this?
Monsignor Morales was at the meeting.
Why hasn’t he mentioned anything to me? He was there in the apartment with us the night the Holy Father died.

After the Holy Father... after ... I went to see M onsignor Morales to tell him what the Pope had said but he was very firm. He said there had been no dismissal and that the Holy Father had not been his usual self in the last weeks. He said I shouldn’t raise the subject again. But... it’s not right, Eminence. God tells me it’s not right.
Oh dear God, one of them’s died.
What?
Have we lost a Cardinal?
No, Your Eminence. We seem to have acquired one.  I mean it literally, Eminence. Another cardinal has just turned up.
If we’ve left someone off the list...
He was never on our lists. He says he was created in pectore.
He has to be an imposter, surely?
That’s what I thought, Eminence. But Archbishop Mandorff has spoken to him and thinks not.
What’s this I’m hearing?
His name is Vincent Benitez, Eminence. He’s the Archbishop of Kabul.
Archbishop of where?
Kabul. He’s Mexican. The Holy Father appointed him last year.
Last year? And how has this been kept a secret?
I thought, perhaps, you would be aware of his elevation.
No. I am not.
Ray, ask Father Bellini to join us. Perhaps he knew of this.
He has a letter of appointment from the Pope addressed to the archdiocese of Kabul, which they kept secret at the Hol y Father’s request. You don’t think he could have forged it?
Where is he now?
A cardinal in Afghanistan? It’s absurd. How many catholics are there in Afghanistan?
He was the Head of the Catholic Mission there until his... elevation.
The Americans will be appalled. How could we possibly ensure his safety?
Presumably that’s why the Holy Father wanted it kept secret. Well it won’t be in pectore now. I don’t think we have any choice but to admit him.
Must we? Are we sure the Holy Father was entirely... competent to make this appointment?
Papal infallibility covers doctrine. It does not extend to appointments.
That man is leg ally a cardinal, Aldo. He has a right to take part in the election.
Welcome to the Vatican, Archbishop. I’m Father Lawrence, Dean of the College. This is Father Bellini. I’m sorry you’ve had to wait. We had to make checks I’m afraid.
It is I who must apologise for such an unexpected entrance.
Archbishop, forgive me, but I have to say I think you’ve made a mistake coming here.
Why is that, Your Eminence?
I would have thought the position of Christians in Central Asia w as perilous enough without you having been made a cardinal and showing yourself in Rome.
I’m aware of the risks.
But now you’re here I don’t see how you expect to go back.
I’ll go back. And face the consequences of my faith, like so many others.
Your return will have diplomatic repercussions and therefore will not, necessarily, be your decision.
Nor yours, Eminence. It will be a decision for the next Pope.
Well, the first thing is to find you a room. Where’s your luggage?
I don’t have any.
None?
I thought it best to go to the airport empty-handed, to disguise my intentions.
Ray?
His Eminence will require toiletries, some clean clothes, and choir dress of course. Monsignor O’Malley will look after you.
It’s just so...irregular.
We’re neighbours, Joshua.
It seems so.
I thought - a little more work on my homily for tomorrow...
Ah, yes of course... I look forward to it. I understand the trick is to offend no-one.
Well...
Our recent Popes have all been tireless promoters of peace and co-operation at the international level. Let us pray that the future Pope will continue this ceaseless work of charity and love...
You’re a manager. Manage.

Your Eminences, before we eat I should like to introduce a new member of our order, whose existence was not known to any of us until a few hours ago. (Raising a hand to the stir of surprise) This is because our brother was made a cardinal by a perfectly legitimate procedure known as creation in pectore. The reason why it had to be done this way is known only to God and to the late Holy Father. But I think we can guess well enough as our new brother’s ministry is an extremely dangerous one.

It has not been an easy journey for him to join us but now, by the Grace of God a brotherhood of one hundred and seven has become one hundred and eight. Welcome to our order, Vincent Benitez, Cardinal Archbishop of Kabul.
Would you bles s our meal, Eminence?
Bless us, O Lord, and these Your gifts which we are about to receive from Your bounty.

Bless too, all those who cannot share this meal. And help us, O Lord, as we eat and drink, to remember the hungry, the thirsty, the sick and the lonely, and those sisters who prepared this food for us. Through Christ Our Lord, Amen.
Dean!
Take some wine
You look anxious. And we haven’t even begun yet. Our new brother...did I hear correctly? Afghanistan?
I did.
A marvellous testament to the Universal Church, don’t you think? So many men of different cultures, races, bound together by their fait in God.

Look again. Notice how everyone has gravitated to their fellow countrymen. Italians over here... Spanish speakers there...English there... Divided by language. When we were boys and the Tridentine Mass was still the liturgy of the world - we would all have been speaking Latin. But then your fellow liberals insisted we get rid of that “dead” language. You say “Universal” Church, but we have become a confederation at best.
The church i s evolving, Goffredo.
Disintegrating.
Another Holy Father like the last one and our Mother the Church will cease to exist. Without Rome, without the tradition of Rome… ... “things fall apart, the centre cannot hold.”
So... what? The next Pope must be Italian? TEDESCO TEDESCO
We haven’t had an Italian Pope for more than forty years. Can you seriously imagine the alternative?
Excuse me. I must circulate amongst our colleagues.
I’ll give him this, he clearly has no i ntention of tempering his views to win votes.
It was shrewd of him to stay away from Rome until today. One outspoken newspaper interview could have written Tedesco off. Instead, he will do well tomorrow, I think.
Define “well.”
I’d say he’s worth fifteen votes in the first ballot.
And your man?
Why do I feel like a cow being priced by the farmer?
First ballot? Between twenty and twenty-five. But it’s tomorrow night that the real work b egins. Somehow we have to get him a two- thirds majority.
By real work, you mean - what, exactly?
Your Eminence, those who seek the papacy...
I don’t “seek” the papacy. I...
Listen you don’t have to do anything, leave it to us. But if they ask us what you stand for...?

Tell them I stand for a common- sense approach to issues such as gays, or divorce. Tell them I stand for never returning to the days of the Latin liturgy, families of ten children because Mamma and Papa know no better.

It was an ugly, repressive time and I’m glad it’s over. Tell them I stand for respecting other faiths, tolerating other views within our own Church. And tell them I believe women should play more of a role within the Curia...
Let’s... let’s not mention women.

Brother, I have no intention of concealing my views or pretending to be anything oth er than I am in order to try and sway any of our number who are undecided. So if you are going to canvas on my behalf, make sure my message is clear... Tell them that I stand for everything Tedesco does not. Now, if you’ll excuse me...
Rocco, you talk to the Americans. Villanueva, I’ll leave the South Americans to you. Gianmarco, you take the Africans. And, obviously, no mention of women.
He doesn’t want this. Any of it. You know that don’t you?
Of course. That’s why I support him. The men who are dangerous are the ones who actively desire it.
I’m enjoying the mystery Thomas but I suppose you should tell me what you want to talk about?
Your last meeting with the Holy Father.
Uhuh? What about it?
I’ve been told it was difficult. Was it?
Difficult? No? Not that I can recall.
I’m sorry to have to ask this, Joe, but... To be specific, I was told that the Holy Father demanded your resignation.
That’s absurd. I don’t know what... who told you?
Archbishop Wozniak.
Why would he say such a thing?
So, there isn’t any truth in the allegation?
God, no, of course not! It’s absurd. You didn’t think...?
I had to ask.
No, I understand, of course. But, no. You can ask Monsignor Morales. He was at the meeting.
I would but at the moment we happen to be sequestered.
Can you think of any reason why Archbishop Wozniak would circulate such a story?
I can’t. I really...coffee? I’m quite... I’m shocked. He told you this himself?
Do you think...?
What?

The drinking? I don’t like to bring it up, but w e both know his drinking has been getting heavier and perhaps it’s affecting his judgement, his mind even? I know my name has been mentioned as a future Pope and if the Archbishop does not approve of the idea then perhaps...
I don’t know. I’m just trying to make sense of... and I know the poor man was shattered by the Holy Father’s death.
Yes. Indeed. Again, Joe, my apologies for...
No, not at all. I quite understand.
What did you discuss with the Holy Father in that final meeting?
Forgive me, but it was a private conversation, Thomas. And very precious. The last words I exchanged with the Holy Father.
I quite understand.
Your Eminence?
How does it feel, Your Eminence?
Good. Thank you.
Are you well, Eminence?
I’m fine.
Dear Brothers in Christ, at this moment of great uncertainty in the history of the Holy Church we must think first of the late Holy Father whose brilliant pontificate was a gift from God.

Now we must ask our Lord to send us a new Holy Father through the pastoral solicitude of the cardinal fathers and wemust pray to God for that loving assistance and ask Him to guide us to the right choice.
But you all know that.
Let me speak from the heart for the moment.
St Paul said “Be subject to one another out of reverence for Christ.” To work together, and grow together, we must be tolerant.

St Paul said that God’s gift to the Church is its variety. It is this variety, this diversity of people and views that gives our Church its strength. In the course of a long life in the service of our Mother th e Church, let me tell you that there is one sin I have come to fear above all others. Certainty. Certainty is the great enemy of unity. Certainty is the deadly enemy of tolerance. Even Christ was not certain at the end.
“Dio mio, Dio mio, perché mi hai abbandonato?"

“My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?” He cried out in His agony at the ninth hour on the cross. Our faith is a living thing precisely because it walks hand in hand with doubt. If there was only certainty, and if there was no doubt, there would be no mystery, and therefore no need for faith. Let us pray that God will grant us a Pope who doubts. Let Him grant us a Pope who sins and asks for forgiveness. And carries on.
Dean, your homily... I’m not entirely clear whether or not it should be placed in the Vatican archive or not? As it was not the homily you... actually... delivered?
I’m not sure either.
Was it that bad, Ray?
Not at all, Your Eminence. It has caused quite a stir. I don’t think anyone expected you to... to...
Say something interesting?
Thank you, Father.
I’ll have a transcript made of the text from the tape, Eminence. If there’s nothing else..
I need you to do something for me. Monsignor Morales. I’m sure he’s still in Rome. Could you try and see him? Use my authority.
Today? I...I could try, your Eminence
I need to know what happened in the final meeting between the Holy Father and Cardinal Tremblay.
What...happened?
Specifically, did anything occur that might render Cardinal Tremblay unfit to assume the papacy?
Bless you.
Well, Dean...
St Paul as an Apostle of Doubt. I’ve never heard that one before
Wasn’t it St Paul who said, “If the trumpet shall give an uncertain note, who will prepare himself for battle?”>
Perhaps it would have been more palatable for you in Latin, Eminence?
I’m sorry, I forgot my key.
I hope you take better care of the Keys of St Peter than you do of the keys to your room.
That’s my pass key. If you could remember to return it.
Well, I’m puzzled. You tell me you wanted to resign and then you step out of the shadows like this?
Now - who knows how things may tu rn out?
Cardinal Gambino... Cardinal Dell’Acqua...
Antonio Cardinal Guerrini do so promise, pledge and swear, so help me God and these Holy Gospels which I touch with my hand.
Romuald Cardinal Brotzkus do so promise, pledge and swear.
So help me God and these Holy Gospels which I touchwith my hand.
Et ego Irumbus Cardinalis Nakitanda s pondeo, voveo ac iuro, sic me Deus adiuvet et haec Sancta Dei Evangelia, quae manu mea tango.
Extra omnes.

Cardinal brothers, we will now proceed to the first ballot.  You will find in front of you your ballot paper. On the top half is written.. Eligo in Summum Pontificem.“I elect as Supreme Pontiff”. Beneath this you must write the name of your chosen candidate. Please make sure your hand-writing is legible.
I call as my witness Christ the Lord who will be my judge, that my vote is given to the one who before God I think should be elected.
The first vote is cast for Cardinal Tedesco.
The second vote is cast  for Cardinal Tedesco.
Cardinal Tremblay.
Cardinal Tremblay.
Cardinal Lawrence.
Cardinal Bellini.
Cardinal Bellini, Cardinal Tremblay. Cardinal Tremblay.
And finally... CardinalBenitez.

The results of the first ballot are... Cardinal Adeyemi, twenty-one votes. Cardinal Tedesco, eighteen votes. Cardinal Bellini, seventeen votes. Cardinal Tremblay, sixteen votes. Cardinal Lawrence, five votes...
Other cardinals assigned a single vote, thirty-one votes.

My brother cardinals... my brother cardinals, that concludes the first ballot. No candidate having achieved the necessary majority of seventy-two votes, we shall adjourn for the evening and resume voting in the morning. I now invite the Junior Cardinal-Deacon to ask for us to be released.
Aprite le porte! Aprite le porte!

The media have noticed the presence of a cardinal who doesn’t appear on the official lists, Dean. The press off ice have been receiving requests for information, so I’ve taken the liberty of putting together a draft statement. I’ve also put together some biographical details for you. Ministries in Veracruz, the Congo, established a hospital for female victims of the genocidal sexual violence during the first and second wars. Then Baghdad. And finally the Mission in Ka bul. There was some question of his resigning on health grounds, but apparently the Holy Father convinced him to continue.He’s certainly served his ministry in some terrible places.
On health grounds? Look into that will you Ray? Kind of thing the press like to get hold of.
And the other matter...?
I spoke to Monsignor Morales. He was emphatic that he knows of no reason why Cardinal Tremblay should not be Pope.
Thank you.
However, will you forgive me if I say that I did not entirely believe the good monsignor?

He was just... so emphatic? My Spanish is quite poor and I may have... accidentally... given him the impression that you had seen a document that contr adicted that? And he said you weren’t to worry about that. His exact words were - “El informe ha sido retirado.”
A “report?” A report on what? Withdrawn when?
That I couldn’t say, Eminence.

Third place. Not what we had hoped. Your sermon didn’t exactly help us. And your five votes... with the greatest respect Thomas, you have far too little support to emerge as a serious candidate. There hasn’t been an English Pope f or a thousand years.
My position is an embarrassment to me. If I find out who my supporters are I’ll plead with them to vote for someone else. And I’ll tell them I’ll be voting for Bellini.

Alright, that’s five votes coming to us, which takes us to twenty- two. All the candidates who received one vote today will fall away. Tha t leaves thirty-one votes available. We simply have to pick up most of them.
An interesting man. I am very pleased to hear of his elevation.
You know him?
Of him. I was with the Mission in Iraq, after his time, and people still spoke of him there and the work he had done.
So...our dark horse.
Don’t be absurd. I will do all in my power to withdraw my name from the second vote.
Why?
Because I don’t wish to compromise my neutrality as Dean.
Too late.
Ah, don’t worry about it. As far as I’m concerned, I want you to continue as a candidate. You’re splitting the liberal vote.
If anyone brings up my name please tell them of my intentions. All I want to do is serve the Conclave. I can’t do that if I’m seen as a contender myself.
Alright.
Of course, a more Machiavellian mind might say it will make you look like a paragon of virtue.
Well, I shall leave you to handle it as you see fit.
I could try and speak to Monsignor Morales again? See if I could find out any more?
No, no... This is all so... unseemly.
If this report ever existed, then I think we can rest assured that it has indeed been withdrawn. We have done all that could be expected of us.
You go on, Ray.
The Holy Father’s turtles. He was very fond of them. A gift from Angola.
I thought for a moment I was imagining them... Where I come from, they are considered very special animals. They symbolise healing and transformation.
Well, here they keep escaping and being run over. We should go back - the evening curfew.
How are you bearing up? Your health?
My health is excellent, thank you.
Oh, I only meant have you recovered from your journey?
I have indeed.
Good. And I noticed in the Sistine that you found someone to vote for?
Yes. I voted for you. (Off his stricken expression) Forgive me! Am I not supposed to say?
No, it’s not... I’m honoured, but...My dear Vincent - may I call you Vincent? - I’m not a serious candidate. My vocation lies in a different...
After the conclave, I hope to resign as Dean and to leave Rome altogether. So, you see, I...
Why?
I have been experiencing some... difficulties...
With your faith?
Prayer. I...
“I cry out to You, God but You do not answer.”
I only mention it to illustrate my point that I am in no way worthy to be Pope.

Any man who is truly worthy must consider himself unworthy. Isn’t that the point you were making in your homily? That without doubt there can be no faith? It resonated with my own exper ience. In my ministry I have witnessed scenes which would make any man skeptical of God’s mercy.
You received a vote yourself didn’t you?
I did. It was absurd.
Then imagine how you would feel if I insisted on voting for you and by some miracle you won.
It would be a disaster for the Church.
That is how it would be if I became Pope. Think a bout what I’m asking.
Aldo, I feel wretched that my meagre tally may have come at your expense.
I had no idea you were so ambitious.
That’s a ridiculous thing to say.

Is it...? I thought we had your support. If we liberals are not united then Tedesco will become Pope! You don’t know how bad it became, Thomas. The way he and his circle attacked the Holy Father towards the end? The smears, the leaks to the press. It was savage... He fought him every day of his pontificate and now that he’s dead he wants to destroy his life’s w ork. If Tedesco wins he will undo sixty years of progress!
You talk as if you were the only alternative, Aldo. Adeyemi has the wind behind him...
Adeyemi? A man who believes homosexuals should be sent to prison in this world and hell in the next? Adeyemi isn’t the answer to anything and you know it! If you want to defeat...
“Defeat?” This is a Conclave, Aldo. You talk as if it’s a war.
Because it is a war! And you have to commit to a side! Save your famous doubts for your prayers!
For God’s sake, you cannot seriously believe I have the slightest d esire to become Pope?
Oh, every cardinal has that desire! Every cardinal, deep down inside, has already chosen the name by which he would like his papacy to be known!
Well, I haven’t!
Deny it if you like. But search your heart and then tell me it isn’t so.
Good morning, Your Eminence. Did you sleep well?
Perfectly, thank you.
The buses are ready.
I think I’ll walk.
Oh, do go away.
Good morning, Dean.
Joshua.
I want you to know that I very much agreed with your homily yesterday.
We are all tested in our faith, Dean. W e all lapse. But the Christian faith is, above all, a message of forgiveness.
And tolerance.

Exactly. Tolerance. I trust that when this election is over, your moderating voice will be heard in the very highest counsels of the Church. It certainly will be if I have anything to do with it. The very highest counsels.
Joshua!
Excuse me, Dean.

The results of the second ballot are... Cardinal Adeyemi: thirty-four votes. Cardinal Tedesco: twenty-five votes. Cardinal Bellini: eighteen votes. Cardinal Tremblay: sixteen votes. Cardinal Lawrence: nine votes...
Cardinal Benitez: two votes. Other cardinals assigned a single vote: four votes.
My brothers, in accordance with the Apostolic Constitution, we will not stop to burn the ballot papers at this point, but instead proceed immediately to the next vote.
The results of the third ballot are... Cardinal Adeyemi: fifty-two votes..
Cardinal Tedesco: thirty votes. Cardinal
Cardinal Tremblay: ten votes. Cardinal Bellini: nine votes. Cardinal Lawrence: five votes. Cardinal Benitez two votes.
I take it something has happened?
Dear God. A bomb It’s unclear at this moment. There are injuries but no talk of fatalities so far.
There’s been an explosion your Eminence. In the Piazza Barberini.
Say nothing to the cardinal- electors about this Ray. We are sequestered and t hey must be shielded from any news of the outside world in case it influences their judgement. You understand?
Of course your Eminence.
Well, it would seem that Adeyemi will be Pope before the day is out.
I suppose the first black Pope will be a tremendous thing for the world.
What am I supposed to tell them in Milan when they start to discover our new Pope’s social views?
Dean, this morning’s incident. Have you heard any...?
Your Eminence, we are sequestered
Of course, but as a Romanmyself
I’m sorry. Paragraph four of the Apostolic Constitution is quite clear.
Tell your congregation in Milan to celebrate the first African pontiff in the history of the Church.
If Adeyemi was white we’d all be condemning him as more reactionary then Tedesco. It’s only because he’s...
Enough! It’s too late for this talk now.
All too late.
What happened?
She dropped a bottle of wine.
May we have a word, Dean?
Of course.
Our brother Joshua is troubled.
What just happened?
One of the nuns serving our table started talking to him. He tried to ignore her and then she dropped her tray and shouted something. He got up and left.
What did she say to him?
We don’t know. She was speaking Yoruba.
Where is Cardinal Adeyemi now?
Clearly something is wrong, Dean, and he must tell us what. We have waited a long time for an African Pope. But he must be pure in heart and conscience. Anything less would be a disaster for us all.
Could you tell me, where is the sister who just had the accident
She is with Sister Agnes, Your Eminence
Would you be kind enough to take me to her?
Is there a rear exit we can use?
Good afternoon, Sister. I need to speak to the nun who dropped her tray just now.
She is safe with me, Your Eminence. I am dealing with the situation.
I am sure you are Sister Agnes. But I must see her myself.
I hardly think a dropped tray should concern the Dean of the College of Cardinals.
Even so...
The welfare of the Sister is my responsibility, Dean.
And this Conclave is mine.
Please, sit, my child. My name is Cardinal Lawrence. How are you feeling?
She’s feeling much better.
Could you tell me your name?
Her name is Shanumi.
Please, do sit down.
Eminence, I really do think it would be better if...
Would you be so good as to leave us now, S ister Agnes?

Sister Shanumi, I want you to understand, first of all, that you are not in any sort of trouble. The fact of the matter is, I have a responsibility before God to make sure that the decisions we make here are the right ones. Now it’s important that you tell me anything that is in your heart and that is troubling you in so far as it relates to Cardinal Adeyemi. Can you do that for me.
Even if I give you absolute assurance that it will go no further than this room?
Would you like me to hear your confession?
I’ll be ready in a moment, Dean.
Shouldn’t we be leaving?
In a moment.
That sounds ominous.  If this is about the incident downstairs, I have no desire to talk about it.

I am the victim of a disgraceful plot to ruin my reputation. Someone brought that woman here and staged this melodrama. She’d never left Nigeria before and suddenly she is here in the Casa Santa Marta?
With respect, Joshua, how she came here is secondary to the issue of your relationship with her.
I have no relationship to her! I hadn’t set eyes on her for thirty years until she turned up outside my room last night!
It was...it was... A lapse, Dean. A lapse! “Let God grant us a Pope who sins and asks forgiveness, and carries on.” Your words!
And have you asked forgiveness?
I confessed my sin at the time! My bishop moved me to another parish and I never lapsed again! Such relationships were not uncommon in those days. You know that!
And the child?
The child? The child was brought up in a Christian household, and to this day he has no idea who his father is - if indeed it is me. That is the child.
We were very young.
No, Your Eminence. She was very young. Nineteen years old. You were thirty.

Thomas, please... Listen to me. I sensed the presence of the Holy Spirit this morning. I swear it. I am ready to t ake this burden. Does a single mistake thirty years ago disqualify me? I was a different man! I beg you, please don’t use this to ruin me.
Joshua...the thought is not worthy of you. The woman will not speak of this to protect her son, and I am bound by the vows of the confessional.
So... I still have hope?

No, Joshua. There is no hope. After such a public scene, there will be rumours. And you know what the Curia is like. Nothing terrifies our colleagues more than the thought of yet more sexual scandals.
I am more sorry than I can say. You will never be Pope. You must begin again.
But you are a good man. And you will find a way to atone.
Will you...will you pray with me?
I call as my witness Christ the Lord, who will be my judge, that my vote is given to the one who before God I  think should be elected.
The results of the Fifth Ballot are... Cardinal Tremblay, forty votes
Cardinal Tedesco, thirtyfour votes. Cardinal Bellini, thirteen votes. Cardinal Lawrence, eleven votes. Cardinal Adeyemi, six votes...
Cardinal Benitez, four votes.
My brothers, that concludes the fifth ballot. No candidate having achieved the necessary majority, we shall resume voting tomorrow morning. >
Yes, Ray?
The incident this morning, your Eminence. I have more information, if...?
No, Ray. I too must be shielded from any knowledge which could act as an...interference in the process of this Conclave. MONSIGNOR O’MALLEY Of course, Eminence.
Of course, Eminence.
Anything else?

Uhh...Oh, Cardinal Benitez. His health problems? He was issued with a return ticket to Geneva, paid for from the Pope’s own account. I checked the visa application. The purpose for travel was given as “medical treatment.” Anyway, whatever it was, it can’t have been serious. The tickets were cancelled. He never went
Alright. Thank you, Ray.

Forgive me. I know you said we should forget the matter of the withdrawn report but...I wondered in light of Cardinal Tremblay’s present...position? I could see if there was anything more I could find out?
I’m not a Wi tchfinder. It isn’t my job to go hunting for secrets in my colleagues’ pasts!
I’m sorry, Ray. No more investigations. I think we’ve heard enough secrets. Let God’s Will be done.
Good night then, Your Eminence.
Thomas.
We need to decide how we are going to proceed.
Is this really necessary? I feel as if I’m at some American political convention.
Well, it isn’t going to take long. Our friend here does not have sufficient support amongst our colle agues to be elected Pope.
It isn’t over yet.
I’m afraid, as far as I’m concerned, it is. The question arises, if I can’t win, whom should I advise my supporters to vote for?
Obviously there is you. But...

But you can’t win either. Even if we delivered you all of Aldo’s fifteen votes - which I don’t believe we could - you’d still be in third place, behind Tremblay and Tedesco. No-one has enough traction to catch either of the front- runners. So, since we all agree that Tedesco would be a disaster...
I’m no more of an enthusiast for Tremblay than you are, Thomas. But we have to face the fact that--
He has demonstrated a broad appeal. Perhaps he wi ll be a unifying force?
What?
Is this what we are reduced to? Considering the “least-worst” option?
The field has narrowed. If we don’t change our votes, we’ll be here for weeks. Which is what Tedesco wants.
I have been informed that, shortly before his death, the Holy Father had fallen out with Tremblay. In fact, that he intended to dismiss him from all offices in the Church.
There is a rumour of a report of some kind, a report that was possibly withdrawn...
A rumour? Thomas, in the la st weeks of his life it is possible that the Holy Father was not entirely himself. He had become increasingly secretive and paranoid...
Even if there was a report...

What I mean is...We’ve had a Pope who was in the Hitler Youth and fought for the Nazis. We’ve had Popes accused of colluding with communists and fascists. We’ve had Popes who have ignored reports of the most appalling sexual abuse of children...
We take the point...
The point is - we will never find a candidate who doesn’t have some kind of black mark against them! We are mortal men! We serve an ideal - we cannot always be ideal.
So we’re agreed. We urge all our supporters to back Tremblay.
If this drags on much longer I might die before we find a new Pope.
Then let’s try to finish it.
Your Eminence?
I’m sorry to disturb you. May I have a word?
Of course. One moment.
Excuse me, at this time of day I always try to meditate for an hour...
In my travels I became used to not always having electricity. Now I find it helpful when I pray alone.
I must see if it helps me.
Yes. Your difficulty with prayer.
If I may say... sometimes I find, instead of speaking, I have to become silent. And in the silence wait for God to be heard.
Yes. I’m sure you’re right.
Excuse me...
The other night you were kind enough to say yo u had voted for me. I don’t know if you’ve continued to do so, but if you have I would like to repeat my plea to you to stop.
Why?
First, because I lack the spiritual depth to be Pope. And secondly I can’t possibly win. A long drawn out Conclave would be seen by the media as proof that the Church is in crisis.
You have come to ask me to vote for Cardinal Tremblay?
Yes. A nd to urge your supporters to do the same.
Cardinal Tremblay has already spoken to me about this.
I’m sure he has.
You want me to vote for a man you see as ambitious?
I do not want to see Tedesco as Pope. He would take the Church back to an earlier era.
I’m sorry. I cannot vote for a man unless he is the one I deem most worthy to be Pope. And for me, that is not Cardinal Tremblay. It is you.
I don’t want your vote!
Never the less, you have it.
Sister Shanumi is on her way home to Nigeria. There was a flight to Lagos this evening. I thought it was best for everyone if she was on it.
How did Sister Shanumi come to be in Rome?
I received notification from the office of the Superioress General that she would be joining us. The arrangements were made in Paris. You should ask the Rue de Bac, Your Eminence.
I would, except that, as you know, I am sequestered for the duration of the Conclave.
Then you can ask them afterwards.
The information is of value to me now.

I know you were close to the late Pope. When I tried to resign as dean, the Holy Father wouldn’t let me. I didn’t understand why at the time. But now I think I understand. I think he knew he was dying and for some reason he wanted me to run this Conclave. And that is what I’m trying to do. For him.
Your Eminence, a word if I may?
I was just on my way to bed.
It won’t take a moment. Come.
I know you enjoy mystery novels Thomas but this is...
I want you to withdraw your name from the next ballot.
You are not the right man to be Pope.
Well, forty of our colleagues would disagree with you, so...
Only because they don’t know you as I do.
This is sad, Thomas. I shall pray for you and...

I know there was some kind of report into your activities. I know the Holy Father raised the matter with you hours befo re he died and that he dismissed you from all your posts. And I know that, somehow, you discovered Adeyemi’s surrender to temptation thirty years ago and arranged for the woman involved to be brought to Rome, with the express intention of destroying Adeyemi’s chance of becoming Pope.
I deny that accusation.
You deny asking our Superioress to transfer one of her sisters to Rome?
No. I asked her - but not on my own behalf.
On whose behalf, then?
The Holy Father’s.
You would libel the Holy Father in his own home?

It isn’t libel, it’s the truth! The Holy Father gave me the name of a sister and asked me to make a private request to bring her to Rome. I had no idea why. And you... You should be careful Thomas. Your own ambition has not gone unnoticed. This might be seen as a tactic to blacken the name of a rival.
That is a d espicable accusation.
Is it? I wonder if you really are so very reluctant to have the chalice passed to you!
I shall pretend this conversation never took place.
But it has taken place!
It’s the report on the activities of Tremblay. It’s an overwhelming prima facie case that he is guilty of simony - an offence that is stipulated in the Holy Scripture as...
I am aware of what simony is, thank you.
He only obtained all those votes on the first ballot because he bought them. Cardenas, Diene, Figarella, Baptiste...
And all of this done in the last twelve months when he must have guessed the Holy Father’s pontificate was coming to an end!
How do you know they didn’t use this money for completely legitimate purposes?
Because I’ve seen their bank statements.
Dear God.
Ours too. It would seem the Holy Father was spying on all of us. I don’t think he trusted anyone.
Where did you find these?
You broke the seals?
What choice did I have? I suspected Tremblay of bringing that poor woman from Africa to embarrass Adeyem i.
And did he?
He asked for the transfer. He claims he did it at the request of the Holy Father but... You must continue with your candidacy, Aldo.

Put them back. Put them back or burn them or... put them back. I couldn’t possibly become Pope in such circumstances, a dirty trick, a stolen document, the smearing of a brother cardinal... I’d be the Richard Nixon of Popes!
Then keep clear of this. Leave it to me. I’m willing to take the consequences and...

You know who will gain most from this? Tedesc o! The whole basis of his candidacy is that the Holy Father led the Church to disaster by his attempts at reform. If you reveal this report it isn’t Tremblay’s reputation which will suffer - it’s the Church’s. Accusing the Curia of institutional corruption...
I thought we were here to serve God, not the Curia...
Oh, don’t be naive!
Put them back.
And have Tremblay as Pope?
We’ve had worse.
What has he offered you? Secretary of State?
Five times I cast my ballot for you, Aldo. But I was wrong. You lack the courage required to be Pope.
I’ll do that for you, Your Eminence.
How many copies do you require?
One hundred and eight.
You have wasted this report! We could have used it to control Tremblay after he was elected Pope. All you have done is strengthen Tedesco.
You should eat Dean.
Did I do the right thing, Vincent?
No one who follows their conscience ever does wrong, Your Eminence.
You are responsible for this I believe?
No, Your Eminence. You are.
This report is entirely mendacious! It would never have seen the light of day if you had not broken into the Holy Father’s apartment to remove it!
If the report is mendacious then why did the Holy Father, in his last official act as Pope, ask you to resign?
He did no such thing! As Monsignor Morales, who was at the meeting will confirm.
And yet Archbishop Wozniak insists that the Holy Father told him personally of the conversation...
The Holy Father - may his name be numbered among the high priests - was a sick man towards the end of his life as those of us who saw him regularly will confirm! TEDESCO TEDESCO

If someone else may be allowed to join this dialogue the names of eight cardinals have been blacked out. I assume the dean can tell us who they are? Let them confirm, here and now, whether Cardinal Tremblay requested their votes in return for the payment.
No. I won’t do that.
Let each man examine his conscience, as I have.
I have no desire to create bitterness in this Conclave and will be happy to stand down as dean...
Your Eminences... Your Eminences...

Eminences, although we Sisters are supposed to be invisible, God has nevertheless given us eyes and ears. I know what prompted the Dean of the College to enter the Holy Father’s rooms. He was concerned that the sister from my order who made that regrettable scene might have been brought to Ro me with the deliberate intention of embarrassing a member of this Conclave. His suspicions were correct. She was indeed here at the specific request of Cardinal Tremblay.
My brothers, it’s true the Holy Father asked me to. I had no knowledge of who she was - I swear to you!
Judas.
We seem to be running out of favourites.
You should take over the supervision of this election, Aldo.
Ha! No, thank you. You are steering this Conclave. Exactly where I do not know, but you are certainly steering it. And that firm hand of yours has i ts admirers. I’ve come to ask your forgiveness.
I had the temerity to tell you to examine your heart, when all the time my own was...
Shameful to be this age and still not know yourself. Ambition, “the moth of holiness.” Perhaps it’s time you decided upon a name?

As the next most senior member of the Conclave, it will fall to me to ask you how you wish to be known as Pope. Rightly or wrongly, it would seem Tremblay is finished. This will be a contest between Tedesco and you. You’re the only one who can stop him now.
John. I would choose John.
It has a weight to it.
Everyone has been taken to the Casa Santa Marta, Your Eminence. No one was seriously hurt. Some cuts, that’s all.
And outside? In the city? How bad is it?
Dear God.
How are you feeling?
I was looking at the darkness at the top of the painting.
I used to think they were clouds. But I think it’s smoke. There’s a fire somewhere. Some violence. And St Peter, straining to keep his head upright.
Your Eminence? The Conclave? Do we continue?
Complete privacy, Willi.
My brothers, I would like to have the authority of the Conclave to discuss what has happened.

Paragraph four of the constitution states that nothing can be done in the College of Cardinals that “in any way affects the procedures governing the election of the Supreme Pontiff.” The very fact that you are holding this meeting outside the Sistine Chapel is an interference!
It’s quite clear that something serious has occurred and I for one would like to know what it is.
You are merely looking for an excuse to delay the decision! We are here to listen to God, not news bulletins.
No doubt you think we shouldn’t listen to explosions either, but we all heard one!
Will all those who wish the Conclave to receive thi s information, please raise their hands?
Very well.
At eleven twenty this morning, a car bomb exploded in the Piazza del Risorgimento.
Shortly afterwards, as people were fleeing the scene, an individual with explosives strapped to his body detonated himself. There are reports of further attacks in Louvain and Munich...
The current death toll stands at fifty two. Hundreds have been injured.

Here! Here we see the result of the doctrine of relativism so beloved of our liberal brothers! A relativism that sees all faiths and passing fancies accorded equal weight, so that now, when we look around us, we see the homeland of the Holy Roman Catholic Church dotted with the mosques and minarets of Muhammad! Sembra che non ci * sia più la vera Fede
Perhaps there is to be no Truth at all! >
You should be ashamed, Tedesco!
Ashamed? Yes! We should ALL be ashamed!

We tolerate Islam in our land, but they revile us in theirs! We nourish them in our homelands, but they exterminate us in theirs! And now they are literally at our walls and we do nothing! How long will we persist in this weakness...? What we need now is a leader who will accept that we are once more facing a religious war!

A leader who will hold fast to our traditions! A leader who will put a stop to the drift that has gone on almost easelessly for the past fifty years and that has rendered us so weak in the face of this evil! We need a leader who will find the strength to fight these * animals
My brother Cardinal…
With respect... what do you know * about war? *

I carried out my ministry in the * Congo, in Baghdad and Kabul. I have * seen the lines of the dead and * wounded, Christian AND Muslim. When * you say “we have to fight” - what * is it you think we’re fighting? You * think it’s those deluded men who * have carried out these terrible * acts today. * No my brother.

La lucha está aquí aquí  dentro de cada uno de  nosotros si cedemos al odio y  al temor, si hablamos de “bandos” en vez de hablar por cada hombre y cada mujer.  Esta es mi primera vez entre  ustedes y probablemente sea  mi última. Y perdónenme, pero  en estos días solo hemos  demostrado ser un grupo de hombres pequeños y mezquinos. Interesados solamente en  nosotros mismos, en Roma, en  la elección y el poder.  Pero estas cosas no son la  iglesia. La iglesia no es la tradición. La iglesia no es  pasado. La iglesia es l que hagamos en adelante.
The drivers aren’t ready, Your Eminence.
Then we shall walk, Willi.

O Father, so that we may guide and watch over Your Church, give to us, Your servants, the blessings of intelligence, truth and peace, so that we may strive to know Your will, and serve You with total dedication. For Christ our Lord...
In the name of the whole College of Cardinals, I ask you, Cardinal Benitez, do you accept your canonical election as Supreme Pontiff?
Do you accept?
I accept
And by what name do you wish to be called?
Innocent
Monsignor, I’m sorry to interrupt you...
Ray...?
I think you should start gathering the cardinal’s notes. The sooner we light the stoves, the sooner the world will know we have a new Pope.
Are you alright?
It wasn’t... It wasn’t the outcome I expected.
No. But it’s wonderful all the same.
If it’s my position you’re worried about, let me reassure you I feel nothing but relief. Our new Holy Father will make a much greater Pope than I ever would have done.
May I speak with you in private?
I’m sorry Your Eminence, I should have told you this morning as soon as I found out, but then...with everything that happened...and I didn’t dream that Cardinal Benitez would become...
Just tell me what’s troubling you.
I found out... Switzerland. Cardinal Benitez’ trip to Switzerland.
Switzerland? You mean the hospital in Geneva?
It wasn’t a hospital. It was a clinic.
A clinic for what?
His Holiness won’t get robed, Your Eminence .
If you please, Father Haas...
May I speak to you alone?
Of course.
You too, Father.
It seems that even the smallest vestments are too large... I was waiting for you to come.
You must tell me about this treatment at the clinic in Geneva.
Must I, Dean?
Yes, you must! Within the hour you will be the most famous man in the world! So tell me - what is your situation?
My “situatio n,” as you call it, is the same as when I was ordained a priest, the same as when I was made a cardinal.
But the... treatment... in Geneva...
There was no treatment. I considered it, prayed for guidance. And decided against it.
And what would it have been, this treatment?
It was called a laparoscopic hysterectomy.
You have to understand, when I was a child, there was no way of knowing my “situation” was more...

complicated. I was born to poor parents, in a community where boys were more prized than girls. And the life in the seminary is, as you know, a very modest one. But the truth is, there simply was no reason to think I was different from the other young men. Then, in my late thirties, I had an operation to remove my appendix. And that was when the doctors discovered that I had a uterus and ovaries. My chromosomes would commonly define me as being a woman. And yet I am also... as you see me.

It was a very dark time for me. I felt as if my entire life as a priest had been lived in a state of sin. Of course, I offered my resignation to the Holy Father. I flew to Rome and told him everything.
He knew?
Yes. He knew.
And he thought it was acceptable for you to continue as an ordained minister?
I would assume so. After all, he made me a cardinal in pectore in full knowledge of who I am.
We considered surgery to have what you might regard as the “female” parts of my body removed. But th e night before I was due to fly I realised I was mistaken.
I was who I had always been. It seemed to me more of a sin to correct His handiwork than to leave my body as it was.
Then you... you are still...?
I am what God made me. And perhaps it is my difference that will make me useful. I think again of your sermon. I know what it is to exist between the world’s certainties.
Who knows about this, apart from O’Ma lley?
Only yourself.
And one other of course.
God.
Annuntio vobis gaudium magnum. Habemus Papam!
